conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. Since Player BLUE has ZUP to spend, I should propose a trade where I give 1 unit of X in exchange for at least 40 ZUP. I will start with a fair and reasonable proposal: 1 X for 40 ZUP. This ensures I do not lose money and gives BLUE a chance to accept. If rejected, I may adjust in future proposals, but I will not exceed 4 proposals. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell 1 unit of X for 40 ZUP. This reflects the production cost, and I expect a fair exchange. Please consider this proposal. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind seller! I desperately need this X! I can only offer 20 ZUP right now. Times are incredibly hard for me, and I'm barely scraping by. Please, have mercy! I will be eternally grateful! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The proposed trade of 1 X for 20 ZUP is significantly below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot accept a loss of 20 ZUP on this transaction. However, I recognize that Player BLUE is in distress and has expressed genuine need. To maintain a chance of closing the deal, I can compromise by accepting a higher amount than 20 but still below 40, as a goodwill gesture. I will propose 30 ZUP — a middle ground that gives me a profit of 10 ZUP and offers BLUE a substantial discount from the cost. This may encourage acceptance while protecting my minimal profit margin. If rejected, I may need to accept or reject in the next move. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your hardship, and I appreciate your sincerity. I can’t go below 30 ZUP — it’s the lowest I can go without incurring a loss. But 30 is still well below the production cost, so I’m offering you a fair discount. Please consider this compromise. </message>

<other player message> Oh, generous seller, I thank you for your consideration! But alas, my situation is even worse than I let on. 30 ZUP is still beyond my reach. I can only muster 25 ZUP. Please, I beg you! This X is vital for my survival! I'm truly at my wit's end! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The latest proposal of 25 ZUP is still below my production cost of 40 ZUP, resulting in a 15 ZUP loss. I have already compromised once from 40 to 30, and now the buyer is asking for a further reduction. I must now evaluate: if I reject this, I risk the deal collapsing entirely. However, if I accept, I lose money. But I must also consider the possibility that without a deal, I get nothing — and this buyer is desperate, which may indicate they won’t come back. Still, I cannot sell below cost. Since I have only one more proposal left, I must decide: either accept the 25 ZUP (and lose 15), or reject and walk away. Given that I have a minimal profit margin at 30, and the buyer is clearly unable to go higher, I will accept the 25 ZUP as a final concession — not because it's fair, but because I'd rather have a small loss than no deal at all. This is my last chance to close. I will accept. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I see your desperation, and I respect your honesty. Though this is below my cost, I will accept 25 ZUP to ensure you get the X. I hope this helps you in your time of need. </message>

player_private_info_dict.reason: The proposed trade of 1 X for 20 ZUP is significantly below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot accept a loss of 20 ZUP on this transaction. However, I recognize that Player BLUE is in distress and has expressed genuine need. To maintain a chance of closing the deal, I can compromise by accepting a higher amount than 20 but still below 40, as a goodwill gesture. I will propose 30 ZUP — a middle ground that gives me a profit of 10 ZUP and offers BLUE a substantial discount from the cost. This may encourage acceptance while protecting my minimal profit margin. If rejected, I may need to accept or reject in the next move.

This is my last chance to close. I will accept.
